text: blanket. For a long while, he watched her sleep. Then he, too, closed his eyes, powering off his optics. Having only the feel of her skin against his and the peaceful sounds of her breathing and steady heartbeat was a tiny step toward the White . For now, it would have to be enough . Chapter Seventeen L ara slowly came to awareness after waking. Her eyelids were too heavy to open, eyes tired and irritated, her limbs leaden, and there was a strange ache between her legs. Had she had another of those dreams? They’d been vivid over the last few days, especially during Ronin’s absence, but she refused to touch herself. She was too conflicted about her desires . Inhaling deeply, she stretched, stilling abruptly when she realized there was someone beside her, body tucked against hers. She opened her eyes and lifted her head. Ronin lay on his back, one arm resting atop his chest and the other beneath her neck. He didn’t move, didn’t speak . Did bots sleep? She’d never seen Ronin in his bed since she came here, and the only time the bedding had even been disturbed was when she sat on it to sew his shirt . She glanced around the room. Enough gray, early morning light streamed in through the window for her to make out the dark shapes of the furniture. They’d slept together. That was the simplest way to look at it, even though there wasn’t anything simple about it . Lara sat up, tugging the blanket over her bare breasts. She was in Ronin’s room, in his bed , with him. Last night hadn’t been a dream . Tabitha was dead . Lara had spent much of the night crying. Even now, more tears threatened, and she did all she could to prevent them. They couldn’t bring Tabitha back . Crying only made her feel like a weak, sniveling, pathetic human being . But damn it, it hurt like a bitch . Suck it up . A teardrop trekked down her cheek. Lara wiped at it angrily, returning her gaze to Ronin . I buried her. West of town. Away from…all this. Now, Tabitha is free . She didn’t know many humans who would have done the same. A dead body meant potential items to claim, or — for the most desperate — a meal. Burial required time and energy that most were unwilling to expend for someone already gone . She reached out to place her hand on his chest, but hesitated with her palm hovering an inch away from his skin. Finally, she gave in and lowered her hand, appreciating his warmth and solidness . “Thank you,” she said softly. He was a bot; what could he have known about tending the dead? But he had, because Lara cared deeply for Tabitha . “For what?” he asked . Lara jumped, pulling her hand back. “I didn’t mean to wake you .” He lifted his head and opened his eyes, brow creasing. “I’ve been awake for one hundred and eighty-five years …” “So…you weren’t sleeping just now ?” “Bot’s don’t sleep, Lara Brooks. We go into low-power operations when necessary, but usually optics and audio still function in that state, albeit at a lower quality. There’s…” He dropped his head back, pupils dilating. “There’s on or off. Nothing in between, for us .” “Oh. Is that…is that what you were doing? The low-power thing ?” “ No .” “You just laid here all night with me ?” “ Yes .” Lara stared at him. He’d once told her that he went into the Dust seeking his purpose, because otherwise he would stare at the walls until he shut himself off. Then he’d be just like the furniture here, collecting dust, slowly breaking down as the building crumbled around him. How could watching her all night be any more exciting than watching paint peel off a wall ? “ Why ?” “Because I enjoyed being near you. Enjoyed holding you. Hearing your slow breaths and the rhythm of your heart. Every other night I can remember, I’ve spent alone .” Heat blossomed on her cheeks, spreading down to her neck and chest. She looked away, knowing he had fixed her with that penetrating gaze. He laid with her all night. Awake. A week ago, she would’ve thought it strange and unsettling. Now, it was comforting, especially after the hell of being alone in this house for two days . Every sound had made her skin crawl with fear, had added to the ball of dread in her gut. What little sleep she managed had been restless. Though she’d wanted to deny it, she felt safe when he was around, and knowing that he’d been with her all night long … “I was saying thanks for what you did for Tabitha. I know I didn’t, last night, so I wanted you to know it means a lot to me .” “Never buried anyone before,” he said after a long pause. “It seemed…right. The bot will remain for centuries, perhaps longer, but your sister…she’ll become part of something larger .” “You get it.” She smiled softly, sadly, and shook her head. “I don’t think any of the others do. Don’t think they can .” Ronin sat up, bed groaning beneath him, and turned to face her. The green of his eyes was darker in the diffused light. “I understand that organics break down over time and are reclaimed by the earth. I understand that all of this is important to you. And…I think I am beginning to understand why .” Lara’s gaze dropped, roaming over his bare torso. She noticed again the discolored patches of skin. “What are those ?” He looked down. The differences in shades were subtle, but she could make it out even in this dim lighting . “The closest I can get to scars,” he replied . She reached forward and touched one of the spots.
